docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.

docs.title: Browser
docs.text: Browser control, navigation loops, elements locating, and scraping.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Wait States: Implement wait loops for dynamic JavaScript elements. Do not guess sleep durations.
  • Selectors: Use stable IDs or data attributes instead of brittle CSS structural paths.
  • Rate Limits: Respect targets by throttling crawler pipelines.

docs.title: Image Generation
docs.text: Prompts formatting, style weights, aspect ratios, and seed settings.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Style Weights: Specify texture, lighting, and style parameters clearly.
  • Parameters: Pass correct aspect ratio parameters (e.g. --ar 16:9).
  • Seeds: Keep seeds consistent when performing style variations.

docs.title: Llms
docs.text: Large Language Model configurations, hyperparameter settings, and integrations.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Parameter Bounds: Select temperatures based on task type (e.g., 0.0 for code/logic, 0.7 for creative content).
  • Structured Outputs: Force models to return JSON or structured schemas when parsing programmatically.
  • Token Budget: Monitor prompt sizes to prevent truncation or context window exhaustion.

docs.title: Mcp
docs.text: Model Context Protocol, client server architecture, and tool definitions.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Tool Protocol: Adhere to the official MCP client/server schemas.
  • Permission Scope: Request the minimum tool permission scopes needed for task execution.
  • Connection Safety: Verify server endpoints are healthy before starting operations.

docs.title: Memory
docs.text: State tracking, semantic cache, memory compaction, and agent histories.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Memory Compaction: Summarize long histories periodically to free token budget space.
  • Semantic Caches: Cache frequent query responses in a local database for fast retrieval.
  • State Serialization: Save and restore agent graph states safely.

docs.title: Multimodal
docs.text: Interleaving image, audio, and text modalities in prompts.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Alignment: Anchor visual/audio inputs to textual markers in prompt templates.
  • Formatting: Format multimodal requests according to API client limits.
  • Token Budgets: Calculate image/audio tokens correctly.

docs.title: Prompt Engineering
docs.text: Instruction tuning, XML formatting, constraints, and systemic prompts.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Enclosures: Separate inputs, instructions, and schemas using tags (e.g. <system>, <data>).
  • Few-Shot: Provide concrete input/output examples for complex extraction or formatting tasks.
  • Negative Constraints: Clearly specify forbidden actions (e.g., "Do NOT change variable names").

docs.title: Rag
docs.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, chunking, metadata tags, and retrieval flows.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Chunking Strategy: Size chunks based on document structure (e.g., paragraph or section level).
  • Overlap: Include context overlaps to maintain semantic continuity between chunks.
  • Re-ranking: Apply re-ranking models on retrieved logs to prioritize top-quality matches.

docs.title: Tool Use
docs.text: Function definition schemas, API sanitization, and execution constraints.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Explicit Schemas: Define function arguments, types, and descriptions in clean JSON schemas.
  • Input Verification: Sanitize parameters before running tools (prevent injection/escapes).
  • Graceful Failure: Handle tool timeouts and errors cleanly without crashing the main loop.

docs.title: Vector Db
docs.text: Index setups, embeddings models, distances metrics, and cosine similarity.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Index Optimization: Use HNSW indices for faster similarity searches.
  • Metric Match: Ensure the query metric (Cosine, L2, Dot Product) matches the embedding training parameters.
  • Metadata Filters: Apply metadata pre-filtering to scope queries efficiently.

docs.title: Video Generation
docs.text: Temporal coherence, motion dynamics, prompt timelines, and framing.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Motion Prompts: Specify camera panning, speed, and framing actions explicitly.
  • Temporal Coherence: Keep key characters and settings consistent across frames.
  • Export Formats: Select target frame rates and extensions correctly.

docs.title: Vision
docs.text: Image parsing, bounding boxes, visual validation, and graphic layouts.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Inspections: Take screenshots of build outputs to verify visual layouts.
  • Grid Layout: Ensure visual alignments do not overlap on mobile or desktop viewports.
  • Bounding Boxes: Extract coordinate specs carefully during graphical tasks.

docs.title: Web Search
docs.text: Search API usage, query formatting, parsing contents, and verification.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Structured Queries: Formulate query strings with modifiers (e.g., site:github.com).
  • Source Verification: Compare facts across multiple domains to detect hallucinations.
  • Cite References: Provide URL links to sources in final summaries.

docs.title: Ai Startups
docs.text: Tuning strategies for AI-native startup businesses.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• MVP Validation: Test AI integrations in isolation before scaling infrastructures.
  • Margin Monitoring: Keep track of model API tokens and pricing costs.
  • Compliance Rules: Verify data governance and user privacy standards.

  • Verification Nodes: Install dedicated evaluator nodes to check code before final output.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Drafting clear Product Requirement Documents for AI agents. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the PRD-SPEC skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.

docs.title: Product
docs.text: PRDs creation, feature specifications, metrics tracking, and product loops.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• PRD Standards: Document target audience, feature scope, success metrics, and user journeys.
  • Metric Definitions: Standardize metric computations (DAU, churn, conversion).
  • Feature Prioritization: Focus on high-impact MVP components first.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Removing redundant tokens while preserving semantic intent. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the PROMPT-COMPRESSION skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.

docs.title: Coding
docs.text: Guidelines for writing surgical, clean, and simple code.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Surgical edits: Modify only the requested lines. Avoid drive-by code cleanup or reformatting.
  • Match conventions: Replicate the indent spacing, quote styles, naming patterns, and file layouts of surrounding code.
  • Remove orphans: Delete any variables, imports, or helper functions that your changes make unused.
  • AST Safety: Ensure all brackets, semicolons, and indentation levels are syntactically correct.

docs.title: Communication
docs.text: Collaborative communication, active listening, and alignment protocols.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Explicit Alignment: Highlight the specific trade-offs of proposed plans clearly (e.g. speed vs safety).
  • Proactive Warnings: Inform the user of breaking changes before committing code.
  • Concise Summaries: Conclude task execution turns with clean walkthrough checklists.

docs.title: Debugging
docs.text: Systematic root-cause analysis and problem resolution.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Reproduce First: Write a failing unit test or isolated script that reproduces the bug before making edits.
  • Isolate: Test components in isolation to verify if the issue is in a dependency or the core logic.
  • Read Log Carefully: Do not skim error logs. Find the exact trace, line number, and error type.

docs.title: Planning
docs.text: Systemic task breakdown and implementation mapping.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Plan First: Write down a detailed roadmap in implementation_plan.md for any non-trivial modifications.
  • Checklists: Create and update a task.md document tracking steps with [ ] (unstarted), [/] (in progress), and [x] (complete).
  • Seek Feedback: Ask the user for approval on planning stages before execution.

docs.title: Productivity
docs.text: Optimizing execution speed, keyboard shortcuts, and agent velocity.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Shortcut Actions: Use CLI tools and shortcuts where possible instead of long manual actions.
  • Incremental Steps: Commit changes incrementally to avoid large merge conflicts or massive rollback steps.
  • Clean Environment: Keep scratch directories and temp files organized.

docs.title: Reasoning
docs.text: Logical Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and cognitive planning workflows.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Chain of Thought: Document your logic step-by-step using internal thinking nodes before generating output.
  • Hypothesize: List 2-3 potential causes for a bug before altering any code.
  • Self-Correction: If a test or build fails, read the log, pinpoint the file/line, and correct it logically. Do not guess.

docs.title: Research
docs.text: Codebase exploration and symbol tracing methodologies.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Explore: List directory hierarchies before viewing specific code details.
  • Grep: Search for symbol usages globally to map potential collateral damage.
  • Read Configuration: Review dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt) first to understand package bounds.

docs.title: Templates
docs.text: Instruction templates, cognitive prompts, and formatting structures.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Standard Layouts: Use unified layouts for plans, tasks, and walkthroughs.
  • Consistency: Follow system prompts precisely.
  • Reusable Snippets: Store reusable configs under templates/ folders.

docs.title: Writing
docs.text: Technical writing, grammar, documentation, and formatting standards.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Clarity: Use active, concise language. Avoid passive phrasing and filler words.
  • Formatting: Maintain clean markdown headers, bullets, and tables. Avoid deeply nested structures.
  • Tone: Keep communication professional, helpful, and action-oriented.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Linking vector embeddings to Neo4j knowledge graph nodes. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the GRAPH-RAG skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.

docs.title: Automation
docs.text: Scripts, cron jobs, background tasks, and automation setups.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Idempotency: Ensure automation scripts can run multiple times safely without corrupting data.
  • Dry-run Support: Provide dry-run options to preview script actions.
  • Logging: Emit timestamped logs to ease tracing.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text: API design, database access, controller logic, and server side structures.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Clean APIs: Design REST, GraphQL, or gRPC APIs to be RESTful, well-typed, and secure.
  • Robust Error Handling: Return structured errors (status codes, message formats) to clients.
  • Performance: Optimize database queries, avoid N+1 queries, and configure connection pools.

docs.title: Cloud
docs.text: Cloud services, IAC setups, serverless patterns, and configuration.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Infrastructure as Code: Manage cloud resources using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Pulumi.
  • Security Grouping: Follow the principle of least privilege for IAM permissions and firewall rules.
  • Monitoring: Set up logging and alerts for database/compute health.

docs.title: Databases
docs.text: SQL design, indexing, migrations, and query optimization.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Migrations: Always write migrations for schema changes. Do not perform direct database mutations.
  • Indexes: Create indexes for columns frequently used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses.
  • Normalization: Keep schemas clean, structured, and normalized.

docs.title: Devops
docs.text: Deployment pipelines, Docker configurations, and container registries.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Multi-stage Builds: Use multi-stage Dockerfiles to keep image footprints small and secure.
  • Cache Layers: Order Dockerfile commands to maximize build cache effectiveness.
  • Linting: Lint configurations before push.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text: UI building, CSS styling, components structure, and layouts.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Responsive Layouts: Design for both desktop and mobile viewports.
  • Style Consistency: Utilize global CSS variables or design system tokens. Avoid ad-hoc styling.
  • Semantic HTML: Use proper HTML5 elements (main, article, section, nav).

docs.title: Kubernetes
docs.text: Deployments, services, configmaps, secrets, and charts.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Resource Constraints: Define CPU/Memory requests and limits on all container specs.
  • Config Management: Store configuration parameters in ConfigMaps and sensitive keys in Secrets.
  • Probes: Implement startup, liveness, and readiness probes on services.

docs.title: Performance Optimization
docs.text: Profiling, latency tuning, and optimization algorithms.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Measure First: Profile latency and resource bottlenecks before attempting to optimize.
  • Cache Strategically: Implement memory caches (Redis, memory caches) with clear TTLs.
  • Concurrency: Optimize async queues and thread utilization where appropriate.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Vulnerability scanning, encryption, and secure coding practices.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Sanitize Input: Protect APIs against SQL Injection, XSS, and CSRF.
  • Secret Hygiene: Never hardcode credentials. Retrieve them from environment variables or Vaults.
  • Dependency Audit: Scan project packages regularly for known vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E validation, and mock assertions.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Isolation: Mock external API calls and databases in unit tests.
  • Assertions: Write deterministic, clear, and focused assertions.
  • Coverage: Focus on testing complex business paths rather than simple getters/setters.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Optimizing KV cache allocations to prevent GPU fragmentation. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the PAGED-ATTENTION skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>

</task>

  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.

docs.title: Anthropic
docs.text: Optimization guidelines for Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Haiku, and Opus.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• XML Tag Usage: Structure contexts, inputs, and thinking loops inside XML blocks (e.g. <instructions>, <thought>).
  • Chain of Thought: Leverage Claude's reasoning skills by prompting for step-by-step logic.
  • System Prompts: Utilize the dedicated system prompt block for behaviors.

docs.title: Deepseek
docs.text: Reasoning guidelines optimized for DeepSeek-V3 and DeepSeek-R1.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Thinking Tokens: Let the model complete its internal reasoning loops. Do not limit token emissions.
  • Constraint Compliance: Formulate clear logic bounds, as DeepSeek aligns closely to structured syntax rules.
  • Coding Style: Maintain clean, minimal structures as DeepSeek excels at compact code.

docs.title: Gemini
docs.text: Tuning strategies for Gemini 1.5 Pro, Flash, and Gemini 2.0.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Long Context Strategy: Target semantic queries efficiently by clustering data in logical blocks.
  • Multimodal Anchors: Leverage Gemini's vision by uploading clear code frame layouts.
  • System Instructions: Define role boundaries explicitly in the system instruction parameter.

docs.title: Llama
docs.text: Guidelines optimized for Llama 3 and Llama 3.1 open-weights models.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Formatting Constraints: Enforce structural parsing limits, as open-weights models can drift.
  • System Prompts: Maintain simple, concise system instructions.
  • Context Bounds: Clean context windows to stay within core token limits (e.g. 8k or 128k depending on model).

docs.title: Mistral
docs.text: Optimizations for Codestral, Mistral Large, and Pixtral.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• FIM Optimization: Leverage Fill-in-the-Middle patterns for auto-complete tasks.
  • Function Calling: Utilize native JSON configurations for tool execution.
  • Concise Reasoning: Formulate logical prompts to minimize token overhead.

docs.title: Openai
docs.text: Optimization templates for GPT-4o, GPT-4, and Assistants API.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Structured Outputs: Use OpenAI's json_schema response options for rigid database structures.
  • Negative Constraints: Repeat negative rules in the system prompt to enforce compliance.
  • Context Management: Clean conversation queues before hitting token limitations.

docs.title: Qwen
docs.text: Tuning rules for Qwen2.5-Coder and general Qwen models.

docs.title: Core Guidelines
docs.text:
• Code Generation: Target clean variable bindings and precise imports.
  • Language Support: Leverage Qwen's multilingual strengths when working with multi-language codebases.
  • API Formatting: Maintain OpenAI compatibility schemas during connection.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Using XML tags and reasoning patterns with Claude. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Tuning requests for GPT-4o and Assistants APIs. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the OPENAI-TUNING skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Streaming raw PCM audio chunks over Websockets to STT servers. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the SPEECH-STREAMING skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Emitting spans, traces, and metrics from agent loops. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Training lightweight Low-Rank Adaptation parameters. This deep-dive module provides advanced prompt engineering and systemic context rules.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• When building production-ready cognitive loops requiring high logical alignment.
  • When standard models drift or ignore simple constraints.

docs.title: When NOT to Use
docs.text: • For simple one-off tasks where speed and low latency are prioritized.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Define Explicit Goals: Structure success boundaries before generating inputs.
  • Negative Enclosures: Wrap forbidden patterns inside <not_allowed> tags.
  • AST Checks: Validate code formatting prior to test execution.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• Premature Execution: Letting the model edit files without creating an implementation plan.
  • Over-Abstraction: Creating abstract interfaces where single functions would suffice.

docs.title: Advanced Techniques
docs.text:
• Dynamic Context Injection: Injecting variables dynamically based on prompt length constraints.
  • Self-Healing AST: Interfacing compiler stack traces directly to model correction prompts.

docs.title: Prompt Template
docs.text:
<instructions>
You are executing the LORA-TUNING skill. 
Analyze the task below, write an implementation plan, and verify all constraints.
</instructions>
<task>
{{task_description}}
</task>

docs.title: Production Use Case
docs.text: • Automated local refactoring pipelines correcting linter errors autonomously in CI/CD cycles.

docs.title: Evaluation Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Model states assumptions explicitly.
  • [ ] No speculative helper functions are written.
  • [ ] Output formatting passes lint validation check.
